message:
fix(http): use runtime timer driver for transient accept backoff

Replace the direct crate::time::wall_now() call in the transient accept
error backoff path with a new transient_accept_now() helper that queries
the current Cx's timer driver when available, falling back to wall_now()
only when no runtime context exists.

This ensures that in test environments and simulated time scenarios, the
accept backoff respects the runtime's logical clock rather than always
using the wall clock. Without this fix, transient accept error backoff
in deterministic test harnesses would sleep based on real wall time,
making tests that trigger accept errors either flaky or unnecessarily
slow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
